methods.description: Process for deploying pollen traps in the field to collect modern pollen rain over a year-long period.

steps.description: Modern pollen rain was collected using pollen traps, composed of a plastic funnel (diameter 140 mm), glass fibre filter paper affixed to the funnel using bathroom sealant, and cotton wool fibre filling the rest of the funnel, held in place with plastic netting secured around the funnel using plastic-coated wire.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble pollen traps using specified materials

steps.description: A total of 30 traps, 10 per vegetation plot (1 ha; 100 m £ 100 m) were deployed, along the 40-m line (i.e. a 100-m-long transect across the plot, at the point 40 m along from the 0 m/ 0 m point of the plot).
steps.searchable_summary: deploy pollen traps along specified transect

steps.description: All traps were positioned at approximately 50 cm above ground level to reduce risk of inundation and to ensure standard height of sampling across this study.
The traps were affixed to stakes hammered firmly into the ground or, in cases where that was not possible, to trees.
steps.searchable_summary: secure traps to stakes or trees

steps.description: The distance from each trap to the closest large tree (> 10 cm DBH) was recorded for all sites, and the tag numbers of these trees noted.
steps.searchable_summary: record distance to nearest large tree and tag numbers

steps.description: Upon collection, traps were emptied of their contents, which were placed into sealed plastic bags and stored in a refrigerator.

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze pollen data, including visualization, abundance calculation, and ordination.

steps.description: Pollen counts were recorded digitally, and sample-specific estimate count sizes required to capture the major features of pollen rain were generated.
steps.searchable_summary: record pollen counts and generate sample-specific count sizes

steps.description: Data were visualised in the plotting programme C2.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize data using C2 plotting program

steps.description: The relationship between pollen and vegetation abundance was calculated by dividing average percentage abundance of pollen in each trap by percentage basal area of the corresponding taxon in the vegetation plot.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen to vegetation abundance ratio

steps.description: Non-metric multidimensional scaling (NMDS) using the Bray-Curtis distance metric was used to ordinate the data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform NMDS ordination using Bray-Curtis distance

steps.description: Wisconsin double-standardisation was used to minimise the effect of rare and very abundant taxa.
The Shannon index was used to calculate diversity.
